title: Foundation: migrate prototype to SvelteKit full-stack
goal: Replace the static Firebase-coupled prototype with a SvelteKit full-stack TypeScript-strict foundation while preserving the five existing worlds, the adventure/mission tone, and the teacher score-following concept. The result should be a classroom-local product foundation with SQLite persistence, backend-managed teacher security, and deep server modules that keep routes thin.
acceptance: SvelteKit is initialized and configured with TypeScript strict mode, with scripts for development, checking, and building.
Drizzle ORM with SQLite is configured with migrations/schema for at least Student, WorldProgress, ActivityResult, TeacherNote, and teacher auth/session state or equivalent secure session storage.

All current surfaces are available as SvelteKit routes: hub/QG, L'Atelier des Livrets, Le Défi des Formes, Le Calcul Décimal, Les Royaumes de Numéria, and Espace PCP / Orientation. Core mechanics/content and adventure/workshop tone are preserved closely enough for day-one continuity.

Activity completion no longer writes directly to Firebase. Activity results are recorded through server-side SvelteKit actions/endpoints/modules into SQLite using a normalized shape that includes student, world, mission/activity, score/best-or-last score basis, error count or error families when available, and timestamp.

Teacher access is backend-managed from day one: no hardcoded teacher password or teacher authorization logic remains in client HTML/JS/Svelte. Teacher dashboard data is protected by a server-managed session/auth check.
Server-side domain logic lives in deep modules under server-side library code (for example src/lib/server/*); SvelteKit routes/actions/endpoints are thin adapters over those modules.
A basic teacher dashboard reads from SQLite and is intervention-first enough to show students/activity results/world progress and support or leave room for TeacherNote creation.
No Firebase imports, configuration, or direct client Firestore calls remain in the migrated app code used by the SvelteKit routes.
README documents how to install, configure teacher access securely, run migrations or initialize the SQLite database, start the classroom-local server, and run verification commands.
must_ask_if: You need to choose a framework, database, ORM, or auth architecture that conflicts with SvelteKit full-stack + TypeScript strict + Drizzle + SQLite.
You need to remove a current world, drop a core exercise mechanic, or substantially change the student-facing product behavior rather than preserving it during migration.
You need an actual production teacher password/secret from the user instead of implementing secure configuration via environment/setup documentation.
You need to change files outside the declared areas to complete the slice.
